question.content: Problem 5: In the circuit shown in Fig. 8-5, find the equivalent resistance between points A and B on the frame. (The frame is made of the same metal, with resistance per unit length given. The number of inscribed equilateral triangles tends to infinity. Let AB have side length a, and each subsequent triangle side length is half of the previous one.). Answer format: a single integer; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign. Answer format: a single algebraic expression. Use the variable names specified in the problem and ASCII characters only; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Construct the infinite nested equilateral-triangle conductor between endpoints A and B with side scaling by one-half.
step_1.key_point: The frame has strict self-similarity from one level to the next.

step_3.content: Use self-similarity to map one module to an equivalent arched branch representation while preserving endpoint resistance.
step_3.key_point: Equivalent geometry reduces repeated substructures to the same resistance form.

step_5.content: Evaluate finite truncations R1, R2, R3 and take the n->infty limit for the equivalent resistance ratio.
step_5.key_point: The converged dimensionless coefficient multiplies rho*a.

step_7.content: Multiply the converged coefficient by the unit-length resistance scale and side length a.
step_7.key_point: Final form is a single expression in rho and a.
